items.concern: Tree-Sitter AST Parser (7-lang: Go, Python, JS/TS, Java, C/C++) with regex fallback (C#, Groovy, Bash)
items.changes: No changes needed for StorageDriver abstraction; embedding text generation is decoupled. When vectors are optional, BuildEmbeddingText remains but its output only materializes on demand if VectorStore is enabled.

items.concern: Data Models: CodeSymbol, DependencyEdge, IndexedFile, RepoSnapshot, RepoIndex, CrossServiceDep, CrossServiceContract, CodeSymbolTestCoverage, RepoLink, RepoSuggestion
keySymbols: RepoIndex, RepoSnapshot, IndexedFile, CodeSymbol, DependencyEdge, PackageDependency
items.changes: Port as-is into a database-agnostic graph schema. JSONBMap remains; SQL-specific db tags are stripped from the Go structs themselves (tags become transport/response helpers only). Models are pure data; persistence layer (StorageDriver) handles the mapping to SQLite or Postgres.

keySymbols: SaveSnapshot, SaveSnapshotDelta, ListSymbols, ListEdges, SaveCrossServiceDependencies, SaveCrossServiceContracts
items.changes: CRITICAL: Extract a StorageDriver interface with two implementations: (1) SQLiteStorageDriver for local tier (embedded SQLite, zero-infra), (2) PostgresStorageDriver for hosted tier. Both must implement the same interface (SaveSnapshot, ListSymbols, GetSymbolsByIDs, etc.). Pulse's code_intelligence_repository uses Postgres (sqlx); create SQLite variant using sqlite3 + prepared statements. The interface signature is the contract; tier selection is a one-line swap at init time.

items.concern: Code Intelligence Indexing Engine (full/delta/reuse modes, git checkout, snapshot lifecycle)
items.changes: Refactor dependency injection: CodeIntelligenceService.repo becomes interface-typed (StorageDriver). Code parsing, git, and file scanning are unchanged. updateSearchIndex() must be optional-ified: it is called iff CodeSearchService is configured (pass it nil for local tier without search). analyzeCrossService/analyzeRouteContracts call s.repo.SaveCrossServiceDependencies() / SaveCrossServiceContracts() which now route to the active StorageDriver. No other changes required.

items.concern: Code Search Service (BM25 lexical, optional semantic via Qdrant, graph queries: callers/refs/impact)
items.changes: BM25 (bleve) is mandatory; Qdrant/EmbeddingClient are behind optional VectorStore interface. Refactor: extract VectorStore interface (IsAvailable, EnsureCollection, Upsert, Delete, Search, Reembed). When nil or !IsAvailable(), semantic search is skipped gracefully (bm25Results returned as-is, no fusion). EmbedAndUpsert / ReembedRepo only run if beginEmbed() succeeds and VectorStore is non-nil. Graph traversal (GetCallers, GetReferences, GetImpact) is pure and uses ListSymbolsForSnapshot + ListEdgesForSnapshot via StorageDriver.

items.concern: Cross-Service Dependency Analyzer (REST/gRPC call pattern detection, microservice topology)
keySymbols: AnalyzeAndSave(ctx, repoPath, sourceName, snapshot) - entry point
items.changes: No structural changes needed. AnalyzeAndSave() calls s.repo.SaveCrossServiceDependencies() which now routes to StorageDriver. Knownservicehosts map should become configurable (accept as constructor param or load from config file).

items.concern: Route Contract Analyzer (HTTP route->handler resolution, reverse cross-repo blast radius index)
items.changes: No changes for StorageDriver; Analyze() returns []CrossServiceContract which the caller (CodeIntelligenceService) persists via StorageDriver.SaveCrossServiceContracts().

items.concern: Embedding Client & Qdrant Vector Store (optional semantic search backend)
keySymbols: EmbeddingClient - HTTP client to embedding service
items.changes: CRITICAL: Extract VectorStore interface (IsAvailable, EnsureCollection, Upsert, Delete, Search, Reembed). Implement two backends: (1) QdrantVectorStore (for hosted tier, existing code), (2) LocalVectorStore using sqlite-vec or chromem-go (for local tier when vectors are enabled). Both are optional (nil-safe). When VectorStore is nil or !IsAvailable(), all vector ops silently no-op. No mandatory embedding dependency in local binary.

items.concern: Temporal Snapshots & Delta Indexing (per-commit snapshots, commit-range diffs, retention GC)
items.changes: Port as-is. Snapshots are immutable blobs; StorageDriver handles table (repo_snapshots) to store multiple commits. DeltaIndexing is a service-level optimization (call gitDiffNameStatus, re-parse only changed files). No special handling needed per tier.

items.concern: MCP Tool Surface (JSON-RPC 2.0 over HTTP+SSE, query_graph/get_node/callers/search/impact tools)
items.changes: Refactor to decoupled interface: MCPHandler depends on CodeSearchService (for query ops, search, impact). Remove Pulse-specific tools (datasource_introspect, canvas app-mcp). Retain core code-graph tools (query_graph, get_node, get_neighbors, search, impact, callers, references). Auth becomes pluggable (pass an Authenticator interface; default impl validates JWT against a secret). For local tier, auth can be noop or file-based token. For hosted tier, use Pulse's JWT pattern.

items.concern: Indexing Pipeline & Job Queue (RepoIndexJob, enqueue/dequeue/claim/complete, worker orchestration)
items.changes: For LOCAL tier: job queue can be in-memory (simple channel-based queue, not durable). For HOSTED tier: use Postgres persistent queue (existing code, no changes). StorageDriver implementations provide Save/List/Claim/Complete methods for jobs. No breaking changes; queue is optional for local tier (can index synchronously for a single-folder tool).

items.concern: REST HTTP API Surface (/api/v1/atlas routes for query, search, impact, config)
items.changes: Design REST routes around StorageDriver + CodeSearchService. Minimal; most logic lives in service layer. Auth pattern: Bearer token (local: ignored or file-based; hosted: JWT). No tier-specific routes; a single binary serves both via config flags.

items.concern: SDK/Library Interface (in-process Go API for embedding Atlas as a package)
items.changes: Create a clean public API that wraps CodeIntelligenceService + CodeSearchService. Constructor takes StorageDriver + VectorStore (both optional). Allows integrations to use Atlas in-process without HTTP overhead.

items.concern: CLI Command Interface (atlas <verb> for humans: index, search, impact, server, mcp, export)
sourceFiles: New; derive from cobra or urfave
keySymbols: Commands: index, search, impact, serve, mcp, export
items.changes: Implement cobra commands for local-first usage. LocalStorageDriver is default (auto-creates .atlas/ subdirectory with embedded SQLite). Flags allow opt-in to Postgres + Qdrant for hosted deployments.

Implement StorageDriver interface using sqlite3 Go driver. Schema mirrors Postgres (repo_snapshots, code_symbols, dependency_edges, cross_service_contracts, etc.) but tuned for local use (no partitioning, simpler concurrency). Use pragmas for WAL mode (durability + read perf). Include schema auto-migration on first run.

items.concern: Go.mod Dependencies for Code Intelligence Core
items.changes: Make Postgres + pgvector optional for local builds. Local binary requires: tree-sitter-{go,python,js,ts,java,c,cpp}, bleve, sqlite3. Optional: pq, pgvector, Qdrant client (only if hosted tag or env var). Use go build tags to conditionally include Postgres/Qdrant code.

cgoNotes: TREE-SITTER CGO BINDING REQUIREMENT: tree-sitter-go and all language bindings (tree-sitter-go, tree-sitter-python, tree-sitter-javascript, tree-sitter-typescript, tree-sitter-java, tree-sitter-c, tree-sitter-cpp) require CGO_ENABLED=1. These are C library bindings; cross-compilation is possible but requires the target platform's C toolchain. mattn/go-sqlite3 also requires CGO. For statically-linked local binary distribution (goreleaser), build with -tags sqlite_omit_load_extension to reduce symbol overhead. Alternative: use modernc.org/sqlite (pure Go, no CGO) for fully static builds, but it is slower. Recommendation: CGO local binary with mattn/go-sqlite3, OR pure Go with modernc.org/sqlite + sqlite-vec (if vector search enabled locally). Hosted tier: all dependencies already include CGO (Postgres is C)."
